A
  • Name: trimal
  • Source: biotools, galaxy_metadata, toolshed, bioconda_recipes
  • Version: 1.4, 0.1.0, 2.0-RC, 1.4.1
  • Labels: trimal, trimAl: a tool for automated alignment trimming, trimAl
  • Web:
    • http://trimal.cgenomics.org/
    • https://trimal.readthedocs.io/
  • Repository:
    • https://github.com/scapella/trimal (github)
    • https://github.com/inab/trimal (github)
  • Download:
    • https://github.com/scapella/trimal/archive/v1.4.1.tar.gz
  • Documentation:
    • Installation Instructions : https://bioconda.github.io/recipes/trimal/README.html
    • General : https://bioconda.github.io/recipes/trimal/README.html
    • General : https://trimal.readthedocs.io/
    • Help: Modes -nogaps Remove all positions with gaps in the alignment. -noallgaps Remove columns composed only by gaps. -gappyout Use automatic selection on "gappyout" mode. This method only uses information based on gaps' distribution. (see User Guide). -strict Use automatic selection on "strict" mode. (see User Guide). -strictplus Use automatic selection on "strictplus" mode. (see User Guide). -automated1 Use an heuristic method to decide which is the best automated method for a given MSA. trimAl v1.4.rev15 build[2013-12-17]. 2009-2013. Salvador Capella-Gutierrez and Toni Gabaldón. Custom mode parameters -gt -gapthreshold <n> 1 - (fraction of sequences with a gap allowed). -st -simthreshold <n> Minimum average similarity allowed. -cons <n> Minimum percentage of the positions in the original alignment to conserve. Output formats -nbrf Output file in NBRF/PIR format -mega Output file in MEGA format -nexus Output file in NEXUS format -clustal Output file in CLUSTAL format -fasta Output file in FASTA format -fasta_m10 Output file in FASTA format. Sequences name length up to 10 characters. -phylip Output file in PHYLIP/PHYLIP4 format -phylip_m10 Output file in PHYLIP/PHYLIP4 format. Sequences name length up to 10 characters. -phylip_paml Output file in PHYLIP format compatible with PAML -phylip_paml_m10 Output file in PHYLIP format compatible with PAML. Sequences name length up to 10 characters. -phylip3.2 Output file in PHYLIP3.2 format -phylip3.2_m10 Output file in PHYLIP3.2 format. Sequences name length up to 10 characters. trimAl webpage: http://trimal.cgenomics.org This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, the last available version. Please cite: trimAl: a tool for automated alignment trimming in large-scale phylogenetic analyses. Salvador Capella-Gutierrez; Jose M. Silla-Martinez; Toni Gabaldon. Bioinformatics 2009, 25:1972-1973.
  • Authors:
    • Salvador Capella-Gutierrez (Person, salcagu@gmail.com)
    • Unknown (Person, tgabaldon@crg.es)
  • OS: Windows, macOS, Linux
  • License:
    • GPL-3.0-or-later — https://spdx.org/licenses/GPL-3.0-or-later.html
  • Input:
    • Datatype: Sequence alignment — http://edamontology.org/data_0863
  • Output:
    • ClustalW format (EDAM) — http://edamontology.org/format_1982
      Datatype: Alignment — http://edamontology.org/data_1916
    • PHYLIP format (EDAM) — http://edamontology.org/format_1997
      Datatype: Alignment — http://edamontology.org/data_1916
    • PHYLIP sequential (EDAM) — http://edamontology.org/format_1998
      Datatype: Alignment — http://edamontology.org/data_1916
    • FASTA (EDAM) — http://edamontology.org/format_1929
      Datatype: Alignment — http://edamontology.org/data_1916
    • Textual format (EDAM) — http://edamontology.org/format_2330
      Datatype: Report — http://edamontology.org/data_2048
  • Description:
    • A tool for the automated removal of spurious sequences or poorly aligned regions from a multiple sequence alignment.
B
  • Name: trimal
  • Source: sourceforge
  • Labels: trimal
  • Web:
    • https://trimal.sourceforge.io/
  • Repository:
    • https://sourceforge.net/projects/trimal (sourceforge)
  • Download:
    • https://sourceforge.net/projects/trimal
  • OS: Linux, Windows
  • Description:
    • TrimAl is a bioinformatic tool that cleans multiple protein or nucleotide sequence alignment. The clean alignment (as a result of the TrimAl cleaning process) is used for phylogenetic reconstruction. TrimAl improves the quality of the reconstructed trees.
